mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates beyond a 0.5 percentage point threshold within a rolling three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the hazard ratio for SPX drawdowns does not exceed 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy expansion that validates corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages concurrently.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both exceed 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During active federal funds rate hiking cycles, elevated short-term borrowing costs compress equity risk premiums and force multiple contraction, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter regardless of underlying earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of hiking period observations, or the slope coefficient of FEDFUNDS on 60-day returns fails to turn negative during identified tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues, creating a mechanical positive drift in equity valuations that persists over the subsequent five trading days as analysts update earnings estimates.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 for three consecutive years, indicating a structural decoupling.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses the 0.25 percent threshold, it marks the initiation of a restrictive policy stance that increases discount rates and reduces present values of distant cash flows, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns average positive after the threshold breach, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and core consumer price inflation signals robust wage-driven demand that supports corporate pricing power without triggering immediate recessionary headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL both rise > 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal generates negative forward returns in more than 65% of occurrences, or the cross-product term in a panel regression lacks statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-pandemic supply normalization regime, rising industrial production indices reduce input cost inflation and alleviate margin compression, mechanically boosting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO accelerates, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% during identified supply-constrained periods.
